content: We can frame the original task-agnostic Self-Refine framework as a foundational scaffold that can be augmented with multiple inconsistent solving perspectives from LLM self-reflection mechanisms, creating a hierarchical self-improvement system that retains Self-Refine's core advantage of requiring no extra training or supervision while fixing its limitation of single-perspective blind spots. This hybrid system would first generate an initial output, then pull contrasting inconsistent perspectives from the self-reflection mechanism to generate more targeted self-feedback, before iteratively refining the output.
